title: ISic000632. I.Sicily inscription 000632
body:
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: autopsy Prag 2018-03-15
1Salvo d(omino) n(ostro) .[---]
2Fl(avius) 𐆌 Gelasius Busiris v(ir) c̣(larissimus) [---]
3praetorium sine ullius [---]
4tot diebus quibus alli mensibuṣ [---]

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: autopsy Prag 2018-03-15
1SALVODN·[---]
2FL𐆌GELASIUSBUSIRISV.[---]
3PRAETORIUMSINEULLIUS[---]
4TOTDIEBUSQUIBUSALLIMENSIBU.[---]

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text from autopsy;
  • 1: Manganaro: F̣[l(avio) Valentiniano (?) Aug(usto)]
  • 2: Manganaro: [cons(ularis) p(rovinciae) S(iciliae)]
  • 3: Manganaro: [damno]
  • 4: Manganaro: [restituit]

translation

commentary

Manganaro suggests restoring either Valentinian III (424-455 CE) or Theodosius II (402-405 CE); Ferrua argued rather for later 5th or even 6th century CE. For Busiris, see PLRE II.243-4. In part it depends upon the possible date range of the use of the formula Salvo domino nostra (attested from the Constantinian period onwards, but more common from the Theodosian period), and also the addressing of a single emperor. ISic000652 was found in close proximity.

bibliography

  • 1980.0512 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001283
  • 1946.0207 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001283
  • Cultrera NSA 1940 218-219 fig.27 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001392
  • Ferrua 1941 157-158 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001456
  • Mazzarino 1942-1943 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10002785
  • Chastagnol 1963 371 n.26 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10002078
  • Wilson 1990 333 n.34 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001509
  • Manganaro 1993 579-582 fig.9 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001827

Broken bar A, D tending to square, lunate E, G with trailing tail, closed P and R. Use of a sinusoid form interpunct after FL in line 2.

Line 1 62 Line 2 60-78 Line 3 51-62 Line 4 45-48 Interlineation line 1 to 2 15-25 Interlineation line 2 to 3 22-30 Interlineation line 3 to 4 32-40
